chunk_summary: This workflow outlines a structured, time-anchored approach to managing pediatric feeding disorders, focusing on growth assessment, pattern classification, domain screening, and targeted interventions with follow-up gates.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise clinic workflow for pediatric feeding disorders, including growth signal confirmation and domain screening.
Learn to classify faltering growth patterns and their implications for underlying causes.
Develop skills to create measurable, time-anchored feeding plans and follow-up strategies.
Recognize when to escalate care based on non-response or complex presentations.
key_facts: Growth assessment should use corrected age for preterm infants and WHO standards for children under 2 years.
Weight-only faltering suggests intake or feeding-skill issues, while weight and length faltering may indicate chronic disease or malabsorption.
A 90-second domain screen should cover medical, nutritional, feeding-skill, and psychosocial factors.
Follow-up gates: reassess high-risk infants within 48–72 hours, expect stabilization within 2 weeks, and expand investigations if no response by 4 weeks.
Consider time-limited oral nutritional supplements per NICE guidelines if faltering persists at 4 weeks.
reference_quote: Pediatric feeding disorder: consensus definition and framework (PubMed) — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30366855/
